data.title: FIFA World Cup: Belgium seal Round of 16 spot with dramatic last-gasp penalty winner
data.summary: Belgium staged a sensational comeback in the FIFA World Cup 2026, overcoming a two-goal deficit against Senegal to win 3-2 after extra time. Substitute Romelu Lukaku sparked the revival, with Youri Tielemans netting twice, including a historic penalty in the dying moments of extra time. This remarkable victory propels the Red Devils into the Round of 16, marking an unforgettable fightback.

data.title: Scientists build from scratch cells that can grow, feed & reproduce
Indian Army's field hospital saves elderly quake survivor in Venezuela
data.summary: The Indian Army's Operation Amistad has successfully rescued a 79-year-old Venezuelan woman from earthquake rubble, providing critical medical care at a field hospital in Caracas. The woman, suffering from a fractured leg and severe arterial issues, received specialized treatment, expressing gratitude for the life-saving intervention. data.title: Jos Buttler scripts history for England, joins Quinton de Kock in elite T20I list
data.summary: England's Jos Buttler reached a remarkable 100 T20 International dismissals, becoming only the second wicketkeeper globally to achieve this feat. He accomplished the milestone with a swift stumping of Harshit Rana off Adil Rashid's bowling. This achievement places him just behind South Africa's Quinton de Kock on the all-time list, highlighting his exceptional keeping skills.

data.title: Empire State Building climb: Couple arrested after proposal atop NYC's skyscraper - watch
data.summary: A daring stunt saw two individuals scale the Empire State Building without safety gear, unfurling a peace banner and reportedly getting engaged at the summit. Ivan Kuznetsov and Angelina Nikolau, known for such climbs, were apprehended after accessing the spire via a maintenance hatch. They face multiple charges including burglary and reckless endangerment for their audacious act.

data.title: Shreyas Iyer completes 5,000 international runs during captain's knock against England
data.summary: Shreyas Iyer achieved a significant milestone, surpassing 5,000 international runs with a captain's knock of 68. He anchored India's innings after a shaky start, guiding them to 189/7. Abhishek Sharma also impressed with a rapid half-century and a T20I sixes record. Despite rain interruptions, India set a competitive target for England.

data.title: Board weightage mooted in entrance exam revamp
data.summary: Following the NEET-UG controversy, the Prime Minister's Office is now overseeing significant exam reforms. The Education Ministry is fast-tracking changes for board exams and entrance tests like NEET and JEE, aiming to reduce the pressure of single-attempt exams. Proposals include incorporating board marks, aligning entrance tests with school syllabi, and exploring multiple attempts and compu

data.title: EPF contributions above Rs 1800/month to be voluntary
data.summary: Employees Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) has mandated a 12% PF contribution on wages up to Rs 15,000 monthly for its eight crore members. Contributions beyond this statutory limit are now voluntary. Employees can choose to contribute more for retirement savings, with employers having the option, but not the obligation, to match these additional amounts. These voluntary contributions can be adj

data.title: Govt asks WhatsApp to pause username rollout
Rangers land Durzi, Beaudoin in blockbuster Vincent Trocheck trade
data.summary: Vincent Trocheck is set to join the Utah Mammoth in a deal that will send Sean Durzi, prospect Cole Beaudoin, and a 2027 third-round pick to the Rangers. The move is part of New York's ongoing roster retool under GM Chris Drury. Trocheck leaves after several successful seasons, while the Rangers add both immediate defensive help and future talent.

data.title: Over 99% of births, deaths registered in 2024: report
data.summary: Sex ratio at birth is improving in some places, but progress remains uneven across States and Union Territories; Kerala, Arunachal Pradesh, Andaman & Nicobar Islands among top performers

data.title: Harry Kane scripts history, overtakes Pelé and shatters England’s 60-year WC record
data.summary: Harry Kane produced a match-winning brace as England beat DR Congo 2-1 in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32. Trailing early, England were rescued by Kane’s second-half double in an 11-minute spell, taking him past Pelé on the World Cup scoring chart. The captain also moved into England’s second-highest knockout scorer as they reached the Round of 16.

data.title: US Seahawk chopper makes emergency water landing in Arabian Sea; one crew member missing
data.summary: A US Navy Sea Hawk helicopter made an emergency water landing in the Arabian Sea early Tuesday, with one crew member still missing. Three of the four crew members were rescued and are in stable condition. The US Navy is conducting search operations for the missing individual. The cause of the incident is under investigation, with no indication of hostile action amid regional tensions.

data.title: Farooq, Mehbooba join call for India-Pak dialogue, BJP says terror and talks can't go together
data.summary: Former J&K CMs Farooq Abdullah and Mehbooba Mufti, alongside nearly 60 Indian signatories, have urged India and Pakistan to resume dialogue for regional peace. Their letter highlights the lost opportunities for millions of young people due to ongoing hostility. Kashmir's chief cleric also supported the call, emphasizing that engagement is the only way to resolve differences and build a pr

data.title: Stolen cash hidden in Ram Mandir bathroom before being smuggled out in small tranches
data.summary: Ayodhya police are intensifying their probe into alleged Ram Temple donation embezzlement. Arrested accused Avinash Shukla reportedly revealed a scheme where cash was hidden in temple washrooms before being smuggled out. Investigators are examining CCTV blind spots, key access to the counting room, and financial trails, including alleged investments in property. Recoveries so far total over Rs 79

data.title: 'Indian-American endorsing Pakistani-American': Ro Khanna backs candidate Aisha Farooqi
data.summary: In a heartfelt endorsement, US Congressman Ro Khanna rallied behind Aisha Farooqi, a Pakistani American candidate vying for Michigan's 11th Congressional District. Their conversation, shared through video, celebrated South Asian heritage and America's rich tapestry of diversity, even venturing into discussions about Bollywood, food, and sports. data.title: Days after Meta Layoffs, Zuckerberg says companies should empower workers, not replace them
data.summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, to offset significant investments in its AI "reboot." While Meta pours billions into AI infrastructure, Zuckerberg suggests a focus on human empowerment could creat

data.title: Microsoft plans another round of layoffs, may cut thousands of jobs across teams in ...
data.summary: Microsoft is gearing up for another round of job cuts, reportedly impacting sales, consulting, and Xbox divisions. While smaller than previous reductions, thousands of positions may be affected. This move comes as the tech giant prioritizes AI investments and cost control, even as its stock has seen a dip. A voluntary retirement program earlier this year also aimed to manage headcount.

data.title: Chargers, Panthers emerge as favorites for Commanders star Daron Payne
data.summary: Daron Payne's future with the Commanders hangs in the balance as he enters the final year of his substantial contract. With a new coaching staff and defensive scheme, his 2026 performance will be crucial. Several teams, including the Chargers, Jaguars, Panthers, and Lions, are reportedly monitoring his situation and could pursue him if he hits free agency next spring, seeking to bolster their

data.title: Lions linked with Cowboys CB as Detroit eyes secondary reinforcements
data.summary: The Detroit Lions could explore a trade with the Dallas Cowboys after releasing former first-round cornerback Terrion Arnold following his legal issues. Dallas has strong depth at cornerback, making Caelen Carson a potential trade candidate if the team stays healthy through training camp. While no deal is imminent, Carson's rookie contract and promising performances make him an affordable opt

data.title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
data.summary: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A&E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments.
